description: The figure displays a temperature-time schematic diagram illustrating thermomechanical processing routes for Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steel. The process commences with solution treatment at 1100–1200 °C, followed by hot rolling (represented by a zigzag line indicating deformation), subsequent cooling, and cold rolling. From the cold-rolled state, four distinct thermal processing routes diverge: Route 1 (green arrow) shows annealing at 600–800 °C; Route 2 (red arrow) depicts high-temperature annealing at 1100–1200 °C; Route 3 (blue arrow) illustrates aging at 450–700 °C; and Route 4 (purple dashed arrow) indicates an alternative aging pathway. The diagram maps the sequential thermal and mechanical treatments used to engineer microstructural features, particularly the precipitation behavior of κ-carbides, in austenitic lightweight steels. The caption explicitly identifies these as regimes of hot and cold rolling, annealing, and aging, providing a manufacturing framework for optimizing the strength-ductility balance through controlled processing windows.
